title: crataegus microphylla subsp. malyana
intro: Crataegus microphylla subsp. malyana, also known as Malyan hawthorn, is a subspecies of the Crataegus microphylla species, a type of shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds, which are known for their medicinal properties, particularly in regulating cardiovascular health and blood pressure. The health benefits of Malyan hawthorn include reducing inflammation, improving heart function, and lowering cholesterol levels, making it a valuable herb for preventing cardiovascular diseases. Malyan hawthorn can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and longevity.
